page: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It includes a massive local storage capacity of 60,000 GB via NVMe SSDs and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, showing strong results for both cached small-block reads at 4502.07 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 219.74 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the top tier, executing 75.3 million operations per second. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, with single-core Geekbench and stress-ng scores falling in the middle 50%, and single-threaded gzip compression showing weaker relative performance.

Given the massive 60 TB local NVMe storage and high memory capacity, this instance offers high resource density for I/O-intensive workloads. The hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for applications requiring massive local storage throughput and high network bandwidth without the need for dedicated GPU acceleration. It is optimally suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, Redis caching layers, high-throughput static web serving, and data-intensive distributed file systems.
description: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and a massive 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and superior database and Redis transaction speeds, though single-threaded performance remains average. This high-density storage configuration is highly cost-efficient for data-intensive applications, making it an optimal fit for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and distributed caching systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 60 TB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory instance featuring sixty terabytes of local NVMe SSD capacity and hundred gigabit network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz
60,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput transfers
Strong multi-core CPU, Redis, and database benchmark performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and web serving
